preamble: The labial weakening of the bilabial nasal [m] to the labio-velar approximant [w] is a historical shift found in the development of Vietnamese and several other Austroasiatic languages.

examples.note: Shift from bilabial nasal to labio-velar glide in specific historical contexts.

sources: Thompson, L. C. (1965). A Vietnamese Reference Grammar.
Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
